data.title: Pharvaris Reports Q3 2025 Financial Results and Updates
data.description:
Pharvaris N.V. ( (PHVS) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Pharvaris N.V. presented to its investors.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Pharvaris N.V. is a late-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on developing novel, oral bradykinin B2 receptor antagonists to address unmet needs in bradykinin-mediated diseases such as hereditary angioedema (HAE) and acquired angioedema due to C1 inhibitor deficiency (AAE-C1INH).

In its third quarter 2025 financial results, Pharvaris highlighted advancements in its clinical development programs, including the anticipated release of topline data from its pivotal Phase 3 RAPIDe-3 study for the on-demand treatment of HAE attacks by the end of 2025. The company also reported progress in the CHAPTER-3 study for prophylactic treatment, and the initiation of the CREAATE study for both prophylactic and on-demand treatment of AAE-C1INH attacks.

Key financial metrics from the third quarter reveal a strong liquidity position with cash and cash equivalents totaling €329 million as of September 30, 2025. Research and development expenses increased to €29.8 million, reflecting ongoing investment in clinical trials, while general and administrative expenses decreased to €9.8 million. The company reported a reduced quarterly loss of €37.1 million, compared to €41.7 million in the same period last year.

Pharvaris’ strategic focus remains on advancing its pipeline, with significant milestones expected in the coming quarters. The company is well-funded, with a cash runway extending into the first half of 2027, supporting its ongoing and future clinical trials.

Looking ahead, Pharvaris is poised to continue its development efforts, with upcoming data readouts from key studies potentially reinforcing its position in the treatment of bradykinin-mediated diseases. The management remains committed to delivering innovative therapies that combine efficacy with the convenience of oral administration.

data.title: Liberty Gold Corp. Reports Q3 2025 Results
data.description:
Liberty Gold Corp. ( (LGDTF) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Liberty Gold Corp. presented to its investors.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

Liberty Gold Corp., a company focused on developing open pit oxide deposits in the Great Basin of the United States, has released its third-quarter financial and operational results for 2025. The company is advancing the Black Pine Project in Idaho, a significant gold-producing region.

In the latest quarter, Liberty Gold reported several strategic developments, including the closure of a subscription agreement with Centerra Gold Inc., which acquired a 9.9% stake in the company. Additionally, the company received a staged payment for the sale of its interest in the TV Tower project and made key appointments to its corporate and project development teams.

Financially, Liberty Gold reported a loss of $6.25 million for the quarter, slightly higher than the previous year’s $5.6 million loss. However, the company’s cash and short-term investments increased significantly to $32.46 million from $6.97 million at the end of 2024, indicating improved liquidity. The Black Pine project continues to show promising metallurgical results, with consistent gold extractions and potential for resource expansion.

The Black Pine project has seen positive developments, with successful metallurgical testing and drilling results indicating the potential for economic reprocessing and resource expansion. The Rangefront Zone, in particular, has shown significant potential for future mining operations, with new exploration opportunities emerging.

Looking ahead, Liberty Gold remains focused on advancing its projects in the Great Basin, with continued exploration and development activities aimed at unlocking further value. The company’s management is optimistic about the potential for growth and profitability in the coming quarters.

Text S.A. Group of Companies, a Polish software company, specializes in software-related activities and operates in the technology sector. The company, which includes its subsidiary Text Inc. in the USA, is known for its innovative software solutions.

In its latest earnings report for the six months ending September 30, 2025, Text S.A. reported a net profit of PLN 59,652 thousand, a decrease from the previous year’s PLN 86,327 thousand. The company also noted a decline in net revenues from sales, which amounted to PLN 167,610 thousand compared to PLN 176,252 thousand in the same period last year.

Key financial metrics indicate a challenging period for Text S.A., with operating profit dropping to PLN 65,513 thousand from PLN 92,989 thousand a year earlier. The company’s total assets decreased to PLN 178,788 thousand from PLN 226,705 thousand as of March 2025. Despite these challenges, the company maintained its focus on product development and market adaptation, particularly in AI technologies.

The company’s management remains cautiously optimistic about the future, emphasizing the importance of adapting to rapid technological changes and leveraging AI to enhance operational efficiency. They continue to monitor the economic situation and exchange rate fluctuations closely, which are critical given the company’s significant USD-denominated revenues.

Seabridge Gold is a Canadian-based resource company primarily engaged in the acquisition and exploration of gold properties, with a focus on large-scale projects in North America.

In its latest earnings report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, Seabridge Gold highlighted significant advancements in its KSM project, including ongoing field activities and partnership discussions, as well as the completion of a major drilling program at Iskut.

Key financial metrics revealed a net loss of $32.3 million for the quarter, compared to a $27.6 million loss in the same period last year. The company invested $52.9 million in mineral interests, a substantial increase from the previous year. Strategically, Seabridge continues to focus on its KSM project, with progress in feasibility studies and infrastructure development, including the construction of a BC Hydro switching station. The company is also advancing partnership discussions with potential candidates to further the project’s development.

Looking ahead, Seabridge Gold remains optimistic about its strategic initiatives and the potential for partnerships to advance its projects. The company is preparing for a resource estimate at Iskut in early 2026 and anticipates a decision on its court case regarding the KSM project in the same period.

data.title: OPAP S.A. Reports Strong Financial Performance in 2025
data.description:
Greek Organization Of Football Prognostics ( (GOFPY) ) has released its Q3 earnings. Here is a breakdown of the information Greek Organization Of Football Prognostics presented to its investors.

TipRanks Black Friday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for the data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

The Greek Organization Of Football Prognostics, commonly known as OPAP S.A., is a leading company in the gaming and lottery industry, primarily operating in Greece and Cyprus. It is renowned for its extensive portfolio of betting and gaming products, including lottery games, sports betting, and video lottery terminals (VLTs).

In its latest nine-month interim management statement for 2025, OPAP S.A. reported a solid financial performance, marked by a 6.5% increase in revenue compared to the same period in 2024. The company’s net gaming revenue (NGR) also saw a 6.3% rise, reflecting robust growth in both the online and retail sectors.

Key financial highlights include a profit before interest, tax, depreciation, and amortization (EBITDA) of €612.6 million, up 4.4% from the previous year. The company’s profit for the period reached €368.5 million, a 5.8% increase year-on-year. Notably, OPAP’s online sector recorded a 10.8% growth in gross gaming revenue (GGR), while its retail sector grew by 4.8%. The company also made strategic moves, such as acquiring the remaining 15.51% stake in STOIXIMAN LTD, further strengthening its position in the online sports betting and iGaming segments.

The company maintained strong cash flows from operating activities, with a net cash inflow of €521.4 million. However, cash outflows from investing activities increased due to higher acquisitions of intangible assets and property, plant, and equipment. Financing activities saw a significant cash outflow, primarily due to the acquisition of treasury shares and the purchase of a minority stake in STOIXIMAN LTD.

Looking ahead, OPAP S.A. remains focused on its growth strategy, emphasizing operational efficiency and strategic investments. The management is optimistic about the company’s future prospects, particularly in the expanding online gaming market, as it continues to leverage its strong market position and explore new opportunities.

Cardiol Therapeutics Inc. is a clinical-stage life sciences company focused on developing anti-inflammatory and anti-fibrotic therapies for heart disease, with its lead drug candidate CardiolRx™ targeting inflammasome pathway activation.

In its latest earnings report, Cardiol Therapeutics Inc. revealed a significant decrease in cash reserves and an ongoing net loss, reflecting the company’s continued investment in research and development. The company remains committed to advancing its clinical programs despite financial challenges.

The report highlights a decrease in cash and cash equivalents from $30.6 million at the end of 2024 to $11.6 million as of September 30, 2025. Operating expenses were reduced, with general and administrative costs decreasing significantly, while research and development expenses increased slightly.

Despite the financial losses, Cardiol Therapeutics Inc. continues to focus on its strategic initiatives, including the issuance of new shares and warrants to raise additional capital. Looking ahead, Cardiol Therapeutics Inc. remains focused on advancing its clinical-stage programs and exploring strategic opportunities to enhance shareholder value, with management expressing optimism about the potential of its lead drug candidate in addressing unmet medical needs in heart disease.

Elekta AB Unsponsored ADR Class B is a leading company in the medical technology sector, specializing in precision radiation therapy solutions for cancer treatment. The company is known for its innovative products and services that enhance patient care worldwide.

In its latest earnings report for the second quarter of the fiscal year 2025/26, Elekta announced a strategic reset aimed at improving operational execution and profitability. The company reported a 1% increase in net sales at constant exchange rates, driven primarily by strong performance in Europe, despite a 6% decline in reported sales in SEK.

Key financial highlights include an improved adjusted gross margin of 37.9%, up from 35.7% the previous year, and a net income increase to SEK 229 million. Additionally, Elekta conducted an order review, leading to a cancellation of SEK 2,197 million to strengthen its order backlog.

Looking ahead, Elekta’s management remains optimistic about the company’s growth prospects, expecting net sales to grow year-over-year in constant currency terms. While challenges such as foreign exchange rates and tariffs persist, the company is focused on regaining market share through innovation and a customer-first approach.
